notes.pro_life: Signed 8 pro-life laws in April 2023 including Gestational Age Act and life-at-conception protections. Stated 'Mississippi will always protect life' and 'Mississippi will be relentless in its commitment to life'
notes.fiscal: Pushed legislation to eliminate state personal income tax
notes.education: Focused on improving educational opportunity and career growth
notes.covid: Removed COVID-19 restrictions early, opposed vaccine mandates
notes.religious_liberty: Regularly posts Bible verses on social media, hosted live-streamed prayer services during COVID lockdowns
notes.flag: Changed the Mississippi state flag (removed Confederate emblem)

profile.background: Finance professional; Mississippi State Treasurer (2004-2012); Lieutenant Governor (2012-2020); became 65th governor in 2020.

notes.second_amendment: A+ NRA rating, pledged to filibuster gun control bills
notes.defense: Senior member of Armed Services Committee, strong defense hawk
notes.fiscal: Moderate-conservative fiscal record per Heritage scores

profile.religion: Baptist (First Baptist Church Tupelo - chairman of deacons, Sunday School teacher, choir member)
profile.education: University of Mississippi (BA Journalism & Political Science 1973, JD 1975)
profile.background: Former U.S. House member and Mississippi State Senator. Air Force veteran and military judge. Appointed to Senate in 2007. Armed Services Committee chairman.

notes.pro_life: Leads Senate Pro-Life Caucus, opposes mail-order abortions
notes.ivf: Blocked federal IVF protection law in Feb 2024, warned of 'human-animal chimeras'
notes.same_sex_marriage: Voted against Respect for Marriage Act (Nov 2022)
notes.second_amendment: Co-introduced legislation to protect rights and privacy of lawful firearms owners
notes.agriculture: Former MS Commissioner of Agriculture and Commerce (2012-2018)
notes.historic: First woman to represent Mississippi in US Congress

profile.education: Copiah-Lincoln Community College (AA), University of Southern Mississippi (BA Criminal Justice & Political Science)
profile.background: First woman to represent Mississippi in Congress. Former Mississippi Commissioner of Agriculture and state senator. Cattle rancher. Appointed to Senate in 2018, won special election same year.

notes.military: US Army Major General (retired April 2025), highest-ranking military official in Congress
notes.law_enforcement: Former District Attorney for MS 1st Judicial District
notes.conservative: Strong conservative voting record, CCAGW PAC endorsed
notes.national_guard: Served in Mississippi Army National Guard since 1985

profile.education: East Central Community College (AA), University of Mississippi (BA Business Administration, JD)

notes.jan6: Chaired the House January 6th Committee investigating Capitol riot
notes.homeland_security: Former Chair of House Homeland Security Committee
notes.civil_rights: Longest-serving Black member of Mississippi's congressional delegation

profile.religion: Methodist (lifetime member, Asbury United Methodist Church)
profile.education: Tougaloo College (BA Political Science), Jackson State University (MS Educational Administration)

notes.homeland_security: Vice Chairman of House Homeland Security Committee
notes.conservative_values: Committed to conservative values, free market economics, lower taxes, fewer regulations
notes.second_amendment: Strong supporter of Second Amendment gun rights
notes.pro_life: Believes in right to life of the unborn
notes.former_da: Former district attorney prior to Congress

profile.religion: Baptist (Brandon Baptist Church, serves as Sunday School teacher for high school young men)
profile.education: Mississippi State University (BS Accounting), University of Mississippi (JD)

notes.law_enforcement: Extensive career: Chief of Police Ocean Springs, Chief of Detectives Pascagoula, Jackson County Sheriff
notes.term_limits: Signed onto H.J.Res. 11 term limits amendment
notes.israel: Strong supporter of Israel against Hamas terrorism
notes.border: Supports Coast Guard border defense and stopping illegal drugs
notes.foreign_policy: Criticized Biden foreign policy on Iran

profile.education: Pascagoula High School, University of Southern Mississippi (BS Criminal Justice, night school), MS Law Enforcement Training Academy, FBI National Academy

notes.jan6: Chaired the House January 6th Committee investigating Capitol riot
notes.homeland_security: Former Chair of House Homeland Security Committee
notes.civil_rights: Longest-serving Black member of Mississippi's congressional delegation

profile.religion: Methodist (lifetime member, Asbury United Methodist Church)
profile.education: Tougaloo College (BA Political Science), Jackson State University (MS Educational Administration)
